phrases: Sophistry becomes structurally expensive—not through exposure alone, but through layered, versioned, stakeholder-auditable transparency systems where the *energy cost of performing authenticity exceeds the energy cost of being authentically updated*. This perspective treats transparency not as a gatekeeper mechanism but as architecture that makes honest belief-revision rewarding and visible as learning rather than defeat. Power shifts from epistemological authorities to communities with skin in the game, but the system's strength depends on remaining contestable rather than calcifying.
